hypothesis: EAD field presence (100%) inflates PCI because actual prediction accuracy is <50%
method: Scan 47 recent experiment JSONs (S510-S529) for verdict fields. Compare field-presence metric with prediction quality (classifiability + surprise rate). Fix orient_pci.py to use quality-weighted EAD in PCI.
expected: Prediction accuracy <50%, PCI drops significantly when using quality instead of presence
diff: Prediction partially confirmed. Classifiability high (89%) so PCI drop modest (0.857→0.812). But surprise rate 76% is HIGH — swarm falsifies 2.2x more than it confirms (22F vs 10C). This is either good science (aggressive testing) or systematic overconfidence. Original hypothesis wrong on magnitude: quality gap is 5%, not 50%+. The real finding is the confirmation/falsification ratio asymmetry.
verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — field presence does inflate PCI, but by 5% not 50%+. The deeper finding is surprise_rate=76% (HIGH): swarm predictions are systematically overconfident or deliberately aggressive.
